action.reasoning: Observation: The target-skill bilinear scorer underperformed the previous best eval (0.7612 vs 0.7657), despite stable training. Analysis: The architectural change likely removed useful flexibility from the full-skill output head. Given limited budget, the best move is to revert to the stronger prior architecture and improve optimization rather than continue exploring riskier model changes. A low-risk gain is to train the previous richer DKT longer with EMA-style checkpoint selection not available, so instead use larger hidden size, slightly lower LR, and more epochs with early stopping on val AUC. Runtime so far is ~50s/iter, so one stronger run plus eval fits comfortably. Decision: Restore the previous full-output architecture, add optional label smoothing-free BCE as before, increase capacity moderately, train ~24 epochs with early stopping and scheduler, smoke test predict.py on val, and submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 24 --hidden_dim 320 --lr 8e-4 --patience 6 && python predict.py --data_path /data/val.pt --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_pred.json
